title: Xtra Citrusy Mexican Fernbrake Quesadilla
description: A lacto-vegetarian Mexican dish featuring fernbrake, citrus flavors, and gooey cheese, baked to perfection in a wood-fired oven.

ingredients.name: Fernbrake
ingredients.notes: also known as gosari, dried and rehydrated

instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the rehydrated fernbrake, lime juice, and orange zest to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked fernbrake mixture, queso fresco, chopped cilantro, salt, and pepper.
Place a large tortilla in the skillet and sprinkle a quarter of the fernbrake mixture onto half of the tortilla.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Place the quesadilla in the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tortilla is crispy.
Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve immediately.
